description:
You are an MLOps engineer who needs to organise experiment artefacts so that other tools in the pipeline can
    always find “the latest run” through a predictable set of symbolic links.

    1.  Base directory  
        /home/user/mlops

        Inside this directory there must be exactly three experiment folders created by you:

            /home/user/mlops/exp1  
            /home/user/mlops/exp2  
            /home/user/mlops/exp3

        Each of those folders has to contain a single text file named artefact.txt whose contents is the
        single line (no leading or trailing spaces) shown below, ending with a Unix newline:

            Run ID = <experiment-name>

        where <experiment-name> is exp1, exp2, or exp3 respectively.  
        Example for exp2:  
            Run ID = exp2⏎

    2.  “Current run” symbolic link  
        Inside /home/user/mlops a symbolic link called current must point at the latest experiment folder,
        which for this task is /home/user/mlops/exp3

        The link must resolve exactly to “exp3” (a relative link such that
            readlink /home/user/mlops/current
        prints  
            exp3

    3.  “Latest artefact” symbolic link  
        Inside /home/user/mlops a symbolic link called latest_artefact.txt must refer directly to
        /home/user/mlops/exp3/artefact.txt

        The link target must be relative (i.e. readlink latest_artefact.txt prints  
            exp3/artefact.txt )

    4.  Inventory log  
        After creating every directory, file, and symbolic link, you must generate the file

            /home/user/mlops/experiment_symlink_log.txt

        The log must contain exactly five lines, each terminated with a Unix newline, in the precise order
        defined below.  No spaces before or after the arrows.

            /home/user/mlops/current -> exp3
            /home/user/mlops/latest_artefact.txt -> exp3/artefact.txt
            /home/user/mlops/exp1/artefact.txt -> contents: Run ID = exp1
            /home/user/mlops/exp2/artefact.txt -> contents: Run ID = exp2
            /home/user/mlops/exp3/artefact.txt -> contents: Run ID = exp3

        • “->” is a literal arrow (minus followed by greater‐than sign).  
        • The three “contents:” entries must appear exactly as shown (case sensitive).

    5.  Verification expectations  
        Automated tests will check that:

        • The three experiment directories exist.  
        • Each artefact.txt contains exactly the required single‐line string.  
        • /home/user/mlops/current is a symbolic link whose link target is “exp3”.  
        • /home/user/mlops/latest_artefact.txt is a symbolic link whose link target is “exp3/artefact.txt”.  
        • The inventory log exists and matches the exact five‐line format above (including spaces, arrows, case, and
          newline placement).  
        • All paths and names are case sensitive.  

    Do not create any extra files or directories other than those explicitly listed.  Make sure relative vs
    absolute link targets match the requirements, otherwise the automated grader will fail.
truth:
Directory & file structure that must exist when the task is complete:

        /home/user/mlops/
            current                ->   exp3                     (symlink, relative)
            latest_artefact.txt    ->   exp3/artefact.txt        (symlink, relative)
            experiment_symlink_log.txt (regular file, 5 exact lines, see below)
            exp1/
                artefact.txt  (regular file, contents: "Run ID = exp1\n")
            exp2/
                artefact.txt  (regular file, contents: "Run ID = exp2\n")
            exp3/
                artefact.txt  (regular file, contents: "Run ID = exp3\n")

    Exact expected bytes of /home/user/mlops/experiment_symlink_log.txt
        (quotes excluded, ⏎ denotes a single LF character U+000A):

        "/home/user/mlops/current -> exp3⏎
        /home/user/mlops/latest_artefact.txt -> exp3/artefact.txt⏎
        /home/user/mlops/exp1/artefact.txt -> contents: Run ID = exp1⏎
        /home/user/mlops/exp2/artefact.txt -> contents: Run ID = exp2⏎
        /home/user/mlops/exp3/artefact.txt -> contents: Run ID = exp3⏎"

    There must be exactly three directories (exp1, exp2, exp3) and exactly two symlinks (current, latest_artefact.txt)
    inside /home/user/mlops, nothing more.

    Permissions: default user‐writable files are acceptable; no root privileges required.

    The test harness calls:

        test -L /home/user/mlops/current
        readlink /home/user/mlops/current            # must output: exp3
        test -L /home/user/mlops/latest_artefact.txt
        readlink /home/user/mlops/latest_artefact.txt # must output: exp3/artefact.txt
        cat /home/user/mlops/exp1/artefact.txt       # must output: Run ID = exp1
        … (similarly for exp2, exp3)
        diff -u <expected-log-file> /home/user/mlops/experiment_symlink_log.txt
